jokes.text: Maybe he's cheating on us with the episcopalians. Look at 'em, with their bright, airy narthex and light, flaky eucharist.
jokes.setup: Reverend Lovejoy is late for Christmas service
jokes.punchline: Treating church denominations like romantic rivals with specific architectural and communion envy
jokes.explanation: Clever religious humor with specific denominational details that shows knowledge of church architecture and liturgy

jokes.text: I wouldn't mind dipping my hand in their font.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Episcopal church envy
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo using baptismal font terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriate innuendo, perfectly in character but predictable

jokes.text: Christ is born; who's on my horn?
jokes.setup: Reverend answering phone on Christmas
jokes.punchline: Rhyming Christmas greeting that sounds like a crude pickup line
jokes.explanation: Efficient wordplay that contrasts religious solemnity with casual phone etiquette

jokes.text: So many little plastic christmases ruined.
jokes.setup: Report of horrible train wreck
jokes.punchline: Focusing on damaged Christmas decorations rather than human tragedy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about misplaced priorities, perfectly fits Ned's character blind spots

jokes.text: I feel like I'm born again again!
jokes.setup: Ned being asked to deliver Christmas sermon
jokes.punchline: Redundant religious enthusiasm that sounds awkward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders religious over-enthusiasm with clumsy wordplay

jokes.text: You've practiced this so many times in the shower. Paper cut!
jokes.setup: Marge reassuring Ned about his sermon preparation
jokes.punchline: Immediate injury from paper, undermining the encouragement
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with good timing, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Did anyone order a hero?
jokes.setup: No one left to deliver the sermon
jokes.punchline: Homer's grandiose entrance with pizza delivery reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer delusions of grandeur with food delivery reference

jokes.text: You know how your paper boy always sends you a christmas card with his home address on it? What's up with that? I wanted you to tip me! No tip till they bring back hagar the horrible. That's not my decision. It's never anybody's decision.
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to deliver a sermon
jokes.punchline: Digressing into personal grievances about newspaper comics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer tangent with specific cultural reference and escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Joseph, you know I have never lain with a man. Tell me about it.
jokes.setup: Mary announcing her virgin pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Joseph's sarcastic response implying sexual frustration
jokes.explanation: Sharp biblical parody with modern relationship dynamics

jokes.text: A pregnant virgin?! That's every man's worst nightmare.
jokes.setup: Learning Mary is pregnant while virgin
jokes.punchline: Modern male perspective on the virgin birth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of sacred story with contemporary male anxiety

jokes.text: That's not all she's full of.
jokes.setup: Angel saying Mary is 'full of grace'
jokes.punchline: Joseph implying she's full of something else (lies/BS)
jokes.explanation: Efficient callback to previous skepticism with vulgar implication

jokes.text: So, not a doctor... well, he will be able to heal the sick. But nothing to hang on the wall.
jokes.setup: Learning Jesus will be king of Jews, not doctor
jokes.punchline: Jewish parent disappointment about lack of medical degree/diploma
jokes.explanation: Classic Jewish parent stereotype applied to biblical context with specific detail

jokes.text: and myrrh, which I am re-gifting, 'cause who needs myrrh? Here's the tag.
jokes.setup: Wise men bringing traditional gifts
jokes.punchline: Admitting myrrh is a regift because it's useless, still has tag
jokes.explanation: Modern gift-giving anxiety applied to biblical gifts, with perfect detail of leaving tag on

jokes.text: This is awkward. The king of the jews we seek is, a newborn child.
jokes.setup: Herod excited about gifts for king of Jews
jokes.punchline: Awkward revelation that undermines his excitement
jokes.explanation: Good awkward social situation humor with understatement

jokes.text: Now you wouldn't hurt the little boychik. Of course not. Kings don't hurt babies. Giant pointy swords hurt babies.
jokes.setup: Concern about Herod hurting baby Jesus
jokes.punchline: Technical distinction between king and sword doing the hurting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with logical technicality that makes infanticide sound bureaucratic

jokes.text: Good king Herod! Except you don't really have the 'good,' so... what do you have, really, then?
jokes.setup: Starting to say 'Good King Herod'
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's not good and deconstructing the greeting
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about traditional titles versus reality

jokes.text: I think my water just broke! You can have the barn. Now feel free to come by the main house for breakfast. There won't be any.
jokes.setup: Mary going into labor, getting offered barn
jokes.punchline: False hospitality immediately retracted
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with inhospitable innkeeper character

jokes.text: I've got some very good news. Caesar just invented the cesarean.
jokes.setup: Doctor arriving for delivery
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic medical procedure named after Caesar
jokes.explanation: Simple etymology wordplay with historical anachronism

jokes.text: I've got the messiah's nose.
jokes.setup: Baby Jesus being born and admired
jokes.punchline: Wise man claiming to have stolen baby's nose (children's game)
jokes.explanation: Children's game applied to sacred moment, mildly irreverent

jokes.text: He looks just like his dad. Not you! I'm not the father, I'm not a wise man, I'm nothing.
jokes.setup: Mary saying baby looks like father
jokes.punchline: Joseph's existential crisis about his role
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Joseph's insecurity and identity crisis

jokes.text: My wine turned into water! Who could have performed such a cruel miracle?
jokes.setup: Joseph trying to drink wine to feel better
jokes.punchline: Reverse miracle - wine to water instead of water to wine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of famous biblical miracle with perfect irony and character motivation

jokes.text: Joseph, no! He has to stay alive till he's 33, when he'll be renounced by his friends and crucified. Sorry, kid.
jokes.setup: Joseph about to hurt baby Jesus
jokes.punchline: Casual spoiler of Jesus's entire life story
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating crucifixion as spoiler, with casual apology to baby

jokes.text: Anytime anyone suffers in the world, he starts crying again.
jokes.setup: Baby Jesus crying constantly
jokes.punchline: Explaining it's because he senses all world suffering
jokes.explanation: Clever explanation for baby crying that ties to Jesus's divine nature

jokes.text: Me? But I'm watching the orange bowl.
jokes.setup: Mary asking Joseph to do something about crying baby
jokes.punchline: Joseph prioritizing modern football game in biblical times
jokes.explanation: Simple anachronism joke showing Joseph as typical sports-watching father

jokes.text: You like physical comedy? Nyuk, nyuk, nyuk, nyuk... A... wise man? Pick two!
jokes.setup: Trying to entertain crying baby Jesus
jokes.punchline: Three Stooges routine with pun about being wise vs. being one of three
jokes.explanation: Basic Three Stooges reference with weak wordplay, relies on recognition humor

jokes.text: I think this nontraditional household just might make it.
jokes.setup: Holy family settling into domestic life
jokes.punchline: Modern relationship therapy language applied to virgin birth situation
jokes.explanation: Modern therapeutic language applied to ultimate nontraditional family situation

jokes.text: Manger danger!
jokes.setup: Warning that Herod is coming for the child
jokes.punchline: Rhyming warning using manger location
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective rhyming alert, economical wordplay

jokes.text: The other tenants have been complaining about the incessant swaddling all hours, day and night.
jokes.setup: Innkeeper directing soldiers to baby Jesus
jokes.punchline: Treating swaddling like noise complaint
jokes.explanation: Absurd modern apartment complaint about biblical baby care practice

jokes.text: What a boffo beginning for my book!
jokes.setup: Witnessing dramatic biblical events
jokes.punchline: Gospel writer treating it like entertainment industry material
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on biblical narrative construction with showbiz terminology

jokes.text: They put his halo on a duck. The oldest trick in the book. What the hell are you doing? Well, he does have a halo. Hail, quackie!
jokes.setup: Soldiers following glowing halo to find baby Jesus
jokes.punchline: Halo transferred to duck as decoy, soldiers fall for it
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy with logical absurdity - soldiers worship duck with halo

jokes.text: What a beautiful hanukkah bush! I say we call it a 'christmas tree.' A christmas tree... what a great name. Makes you feel all warm and fuzzy.
jokes.setup: Finding decorated tree in forest
jokes.punchline: Inventing the name Christmas tree, treating it like marketing genius
jokes.explanation: Origin story for Christmas tree with modern branding sensibility

jokes.text: And did you know... That little baby Jesus grew up to be... Jesus? I know. It's weird, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Homer building to dramatic revelation
jokes.punchline: Obvious fact presented as surprising, acknowledged as weird
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer anti-climax with meta-acknowledgment of the obvious

jokes.text: I don't know why I'm so run down. I must be low on christmas spirit. It's 'cause you're using regular batteries. You forgot about energizer ultra! Now let me crawl inside you. Cold... so cold.
jokes.setup: Energizer bunny Christmas special parody
jokes.punchline: Product placement turned disturbing with battery insertion
jokes.explanation: Commercial parody that goes dark but feels disconnected from main show

jokes.text: What are you doing in the chimney? Trying to kill Santa. What's it to ya?! What do you have against Santa? He screwed me over back in WWII!
jokes.setup: Finding Grampa in chimney
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of attempted murder with historical grudge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa character moment - casual violence with specific historical grievance

jokes.text: Okay, Abe, let's win this war quick, so we can go home and slowly give black folks more rights.
jokes.setup: Brothers going into WWII combat
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging slow pace of civil rights progress
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical commentary wrapped in period-appropriate optimism

jokes.text: Well, I don't usually drink after lunch, but... oh, my god! The sky is full of that kind of plane that crashes into the boat!
jokes.setup: Abe about to have a drink
jokes.punchline: Circumlocuting 'kamikaze' while panicking
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa confused elderly speech pattern under pressure

jokes.text: Good thing I stole two of these off dead soldiers.
jokes.setup: Cyrus showing good luck charm
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of looting corpses
jokes.explanation: Dark wartime humor with casual moral flexibility

jokes.text: I'm talking to my best gal. I miss you, too, Bernadette. You hang up first. No... you hang up first. No. You hang up first.
jokes.setup: Radio operator supposed to relay urgent message
jokes.punchline: Extended personal phone conversation during battle
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor but drags on too long for the payoff

jokes.text: You'll pay for that, you haiku-spouting savages!
jokes.setup: Abe angry at Japanese pilots
jokes.punchline: Specific cultural reference in racist epithet
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate character racism with cultural specificity

jokes.text: I wish I tried reefer!
jokes.setup: Cyrus dying in plane crash
jokes.punchline: Deathbed regret about not trying marijuana
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about priorities in final moments, economical delivery

jokes.text: On this island, military rank means nothing. We're in a state of nature where the strong of will control the weak.
jokes.setup: Abe telling Burns to build fire
jokes.punchline: Burns invoking philosophical concepts to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character - using intellectualism to avoid physical labor

jokes.text: How dare you drown the king of New Burns Island!
jokes.setup: Burns being dunked in water
jokes.punchline: Claiming royal title on island he just named after himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns megalomania with instant self-appointed royalty

jokes.text: Days turned to weeks. Weeks turned to months. Months turned back to days.
jokes.setup: Narrating passage of time on island
jokes.punchline: Illogical time progression
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of standard time passage narration with impossible logic

jokes.text: Hey, sailor, my eyes are up here.
jokes.setup: Burns looking attractive to starving Abe
jokes.punchline: Burns using feminine response to ogling
jokes.explanation: Gender role reversal with Burns as object of desire, efficient delivery

jokes.text: Is this pomade? It's nothing really, just seagull brains and snail goo.
jokes.setup: Burns giving Abe grooming kit as peace offering
jokes.punchline: Disgusting island-made hair product ingredients
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with makeshift island survival resourcefulness

jokes.text: What in the name of the Tennessee valley authority?
jokes.setup: Seeing something surprising (Santa's sleigh)
jokes.punchline: Swearing by New Deal government program
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific period oath referencing FDR-era infrastructure program

jokes.text: He's german. That's good eatin'. You useless waste of socks!
jokes.setup: Seeing crashed Santa, thinking he's German
jokes.punchline: Burns wanting to eat German, Santa insulting reindeer
jokes.explanation: Wartime cannibalistic hunger with Santa's unusual reindeer insult

jokes.text: Damn it! Sorry, chuckles, change of plan. This sleigh is my ticket off this hellhole. Because, at heart, I'm just a very wicked child.
jokes.setup: Burns pretending to help Santa
jokes.punchline: Stealing sleigh and acknowledging his evil nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment with self-aware villainous monologue

jokes.text: You backstabber! And I let you spoon me.
jokes.setup: Abe betrayed by Burns
jokes.punchline: Revealing intimate sleeping arrangement
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable revelation about survival intimacy, economical delivery

jokes.text: Somebody should have beat you with a tricycle years ago!
jokes.setup: Abe angry at Burns for stealing sleigh
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific childhood violence wish
jokes.explanation: Specific unusual threat that sounds like elderly person insult

jokes.text: If I hadn't invented a jet ski made of coconuts, I'd have never gotten home.
jokes.setup: Abe explaining how he got off the island
jokes.punchline: Impossible coconut jet ski invention
jokes.explanation: Absurd Grampa tall tale with specific impossible engineering

jokes.text: Sorry I never called, Abe. I was too busy with my 15 native wives.
jokes.setup: Cyrus reuniting with Abe after decades
jokes.punchline: Excessive polygamy excuse for not staying in touch
jokes.explanation: Typical male fantasy excuse with specific excessive number

jokes.text: 15! Whoa, that sounds like a lot of sex! I said wives, not girlfriends.
jokes.setup: Abe impressed by 15 wives
jokes.punchline: Cyrus clarifying that marriage means less sex
jokes.explanation: Classic marriage = less sex joke with good setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: oh my god, it's over. We're free! It's not over. It's just intermission. I said we're free! we're still doing act two. I'll be the audience. Boo! This is terrible!
jokes.setup: Audience thinking show is over
jokes.punchline: Confusion about intermission, immediate negative review
jokes.explanation: Meta-theatrical humor about audience expectations, somewhat generic

jokes.text: This time of year, everybody does it, 'cause you don't have to pay for the music rights.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about Nutcracker music usage
jokes.punchline: Explaining public domain Christmas music economics
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on entertainment industry practices, perfectly fits Lisa's character

jokes.text: Why is this Santa suit so snug? Why should I care? It's all humbug I've jacked my prices up so high but there's no junk these saps won't buy
jokes.setup: Christmas musical number starting
jokes.punchline: Moe's cynical Christmas retail perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character cynicism in musical format

jokes.text: These holiday cookies swell my fat cat even though we're not gentile
jokes.setup: Krusty singing in Christmas musical
jokes.punchline: Jewish character participating in Christmas despite religion
jokes.explanation: Cultural assimilation humor with Krusty's Jewish identity

jokes.text: Fine. I'll spend christmas eve with the raccoons.
jokes.setup: Family avoiding Grampa at Christmas
jokes.punchline: Grampa's pathetic alternative Christmas plan
jokes.explanation: Sad Grampa moment with specific pathetic detail

jokes.text: You got me a gift? Rancid lard?! You shouldn't have.
jokes.setup: Kent receiving Christmas gift on air
jokes.punchline: Disgusting gift with polite response
jokes.explanation: Standard politeness vs. reality humor with gross gift

jokes.text: 'Give the audience snowballs,' they said. 'It'll be cute,' they said.
jokes.setup: Krusty getting pelted with snowballs
jokes.punchline: Regretful repetition about bad production decision
jokes.explanation: Standard show business regret format with physical comedy

jokes.text: Okay, time for my annual holiday tradition: Attempting to kill myself.
jokes.setup: Moe announcing holiday tradition
jokes.punchline: Casual suicide attempt as Christmas tradition
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Moe humor with matter-of-fact delivery about suicide

jokes.text: Barn, as a special gift to me this year, will you kill me? But I already got you a wool hat. Maybe next year?
jokes.setup: Moe asking Barney to kill him as gift
jokes.punchline: Barney politely declining due to existing gift, suggesting later
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with polite social conventions applied to murder request

jokes.text: I just have jerky made of trout.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately seeking Christmas gift at store
jokes.punchline: Apu's bizarre remaining inventory item
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific and unappetizing convenience store food item

jokes.text: I knew you'd forget, so my gift to you is a gift for you to give me.
jokes.setup: Homer forgot to get Marge a gift
jokes.punchline: Sister providing gift that loops back to herself
jokes.explanation: Clever circular gift logic that solves problem while being self-serving

jokes.text: 18 wheels, and they all missed me.
jokes.setup: Hans Moleman's typical near-death experience
jokes.punchline: Specific count of truck wheels that failed to kill him
jokes.explanation: Classic Hans Moleman near-death experience with specific truck detail
